text: Welcome to the demo! Let's watch AI agents work together to solve a coding task.

Welcome to GitHub Copilot CLI!
How can I help you today?
⚙️ WORKING — The developer launched GitHub Copilot, an AI assistant that helps with coding. Think of it like calling a knowledgeable colleague.
Press Enter to continue, or ask a question...

> Add password reset functionality to the auth module

I'll break this into steps:
1. Create the password reset endpoint
2. Add email notification
3. Write tests

Let me start by exploring the existing code...
💡 The AI is planning its approach. It broke a big task into three smaller steps — like a project manager creating a to-do list. First, it needs to understand the existing code before making changes.

Launching explore agent to understand auth module...

[explore] Scanning src/auth/
[explore] Found: login.ts, middleware.ts, types.ts
[explore] Auth uses JWT tokens with 24h expiry
[explore] Database: PostgreSQL with users table

🔬 The AI sent out a helper — called an 'explore agent' — to read through the project files. It's like sending a research assistant to the library. The helper found the key files and reported back what technologies are being used.
Press Enter to continue, or ask a question...

Creating src/auth/password-reset.ts...

[task] Writing password reset handler
[task] Adding reset token generation
[task] Adding email template
[task] File created: src/auth/password-reset.ts (47 lines)

🏗️ BUILDING — Now a 'task agent' is doing the actual work — writing new code. It created a new file for password reset. The AI is building on what the explore agent learned about the existing code structure.

[task] Writing tests...
[task] Created: src/auth/password-reset.test.ts
[task] Running tests...

PASS src/auth/password-reset.test.ts
  ✓ generates reset token (5ms)
  ✓ validates reset token (3ms)
  ✓ resets password successfully (8ms)
  ✓ rejects expired tokens (2ms)

Tests: 4 passed, 4 total

🧪 QUALITY CHECK — The AI wrote checks for its own work and ran them — all 4 passed! It tested that reset tokens are created correctly, that expired tokens are rejected, and that passwords actually get changed. The AI checks its own homework.

Done! Here's what I built:
- src/auth/password-reset.ts (new)
- src/auth/password-reset.test.ts (new)
- 4 tests passing

Would you like me to commit these changes?

✅ The AI finished! It used two specialized helpers — one to research and one to build — then verified its own work. Two new files, four passing checks. Now it's asking the developer if they want to save this checkpoint.
🎬 That's how AI agents collaborate! One explores, one builds, and they check their own work. The developer stays in control — the AI asks before saving anything.
